trivia_bullets.text: Trial of Agony is one of multiple Duskmourn cards built as direct references to the Saw film franchise (the 'death game' horror subgenre). Wargamer and TheGamer film-reference roundups group it with Saw (the equipment), Let's Play A Game, and The Lord of Pain as the set's Saw-coordinated cards. [Wargamer: 'Every single horror reference in MTG Duskmourn' / TheGamer: '10 Best Horror Movie References' / Star City Games: 'Duskmourn Film References Explained', via WebSearch snippets]

Despite the 'Trial of' prefix evoking earlier MTG cycles, no second 'Trial of'-named card was printed in Duskmourn — Scryfall set search returns Trial of Agony as the lone instance. The card is a Saw-franchise homage rather than a designer-coordinated DSK 'Trial of' cycle. [Scryfall set search]
Art by Mike Sass. Uncommon. Mono-red sorcery, cost {R}. Released 2024-09-27 as part of Duskmourn: House of Horror (DSK 159). [Scryfall]

Flavor text: 'All it took to shatter their lifelong bond was a scrap of hope.' — written to match the card's coerced-choice mechanic, where one opponent is forced to choose which of two of their own creatures takes the hit. [Scryfall]

Oracle text: 'Choose two target creatures controlled by the same opponent. That player chooses one of those creatures. Trial of Agony deals 5 damage to that creature, and the other can't block this turn.' — a one-mana 5-damage burn / removal with a secondary 'other can't block' rider, gated by the opponent's choice. [Scryfall]
The card was given an early world preview by French outlet Numerama on 2024-09-06, positioning it as a marquee horror-flavor reveal in the DSK preview cycle. [Scryfall: preview metadata]

Community framing per published horror-reference roundups: Trial of Agony is read as a Jigsaw / death-game tribute — the sacrificial-choice mechanic operationalizes the Saw franchise's 'pick one' moral-coercion trope in MTG rules. [Wargamer / Star City Games coverage, via WebSearch snippets]
